content.content:
1	//! Code generator for Lua 5.4.
2	//!
3	//! Ports `src/lcode.c` (1875 lines, ~60 functions) and the public interface
4	//! from `src/lcode.h`.
5	//!
6	//! Phase A: faithful logic translation — does not yet compile.
7	//!
8	//! TODO(port): FuncState.f is GcRef<LuaProto> per types.tsv but the code
9	//! generator mutates it directly.  Phase B must wrap LuaProto in
10	//! RefCell<LuaProto> (or similar) inside GcRef so that `fs.f.code`, `fs.f.k`,
11	//! etc. are mutable.  All `fs.f.<field>` accesses below assume direct mutability.
12	//!
13	//! TODO(port): LexState.L (the &mut LuaState back-pointer) was removed per
14	//! types.tsv.  Functions that call luaM_growvector / luaH_get / luaC_barrier /
15	//! luaO_rawarith carry an explicit `state: &mut LuaState` parameter here.
16	//! The caller (lua-parse) must thread it through.
17	
18	// C: /* $Id: lcode.c $ */
19	// C: #define lcode_c
20	// C: #define LUA_CORE
21	
22	use crate::opcodes::{
23	    Instruction, OpCode, OpMode,
24	    MAXARG_A, MAXARG_B, MAXARG_C, MAXARG_BX, MAXARG_AX, MAXARG_S_J,
25	    OFFSET_S_BX, OFFSET_S_J, OFFSET_S_C,
26	    NO_REG, MAXINDEXRK, LFIELDS_PER_FLUSH,
27	};
28	
29	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
30	use lua_types::value::LuaValue;
31	use lua_types::string::LuaString;
32	use lua_types::gc::GcRef;
33	use lua_types::tagmethod::TagMethod;
34	
35	// ─── PHASE B PLACEHOLDERS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	//
37	// The following types live in `lua-parse` (`FuncState`, `ExprDesc`, `ExprKind`,
38	// `LexState`) and `lua-vm` (`LuaState`, `LuaTable`).  Importing them from those
39	// crates would create a dependency cycle (`lua-vm` depends on `lua-code`), so
40	// we keep stub shapes here in Phase B.  The real definitions land when those
41	// crates fully exist; the field/method signatures below are inferred from the
42	// concrete usage in `codegen.rs`.
43	
44	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-types (`lua_types::proto::LuaProto`),
45	/// but it stores `Vec<lua_types::opcode::Instruction>` — a barebones u32 wrapper
46	/// without the `arg_a` / `set_opcode` / `is_in_top` / etc. methods that the
47	/// code generator calls.  Those methods live on the local
48	/// `crate::opcodes::Instruction` type.  Until the two `Instruction` types are
49	/// unified (Phase B), `FuncState` carries a local proto shape that uses the
50	/// methodful local `Instruction`.
51	pub struct LuaProto {
52	    pub numparams: u8,
53	    pub is_vararg: bool,
54	    pub maxstacksize: u8,
55	    pub k: Vec<LuaValue>,
56	    pub code: Vec<Instruction>,
57	    pub lineinfo: Vec<i8>,
58	    pub abslineinfo: Vec<AbsLineInfo>,
59	}
60	
61	/// PHASE B: mirrors `lua_types::proto::AbsLineInfo`; kept local so it pairs
62	/// with the local `LuaProto` placeholder above.
63	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
64	pub struct AbsLineInfo {
65	    pub pc: i32,
66	    pub line: i32,
67	}
68	
69	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
70	/// from codegen usage.
71	pub struct FuncState {
72	    pub f: LuaProto,
73	    pub pc: i32,
74	    pub lasttarget: i32,
75	    pub previousline: i32,
76	    pub iwthabs: u8,
77	    pub nabslineinfo: i32,
78	    pub nactvar: u8,
79	    pub freereg: u8,
80	    pub nk: i32,
81	    pub needclose: bool,
82	}
83	
84	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
85	/// from codegen usage.  Mirrors the C `expdesc` struct (a tagged kind plus a
86	/// payload union); the union becomes a plain struct here so every variant's
87	/// fields are addressable.
88	pub struct ExprDesc {
89	    pub k: ExprKind,
90	    pub t: i32,
91	    pub f: i32,
92	    pub u: ExprDescU,
93	}
94	
95	impl Clone for ExprDesc {

320	/// In C: stack-allocated in `body()`, chained via raw `*prev` pointer.
321	/// In Rust: heap-allocated via `Option<Box<FuncState>>` in LexState.
322	#[derive(Debug)]
323	pub struct FuncState {
324	    /// C: f — the Proto being built.
325	    /// PORT NOTE: types.tsv maps this to GcRef<LuaProto>; we use Box<LuaProto>
326	    ///   during compilation to avoid RefCell overhead. close_func hands it to
327	    ///   the GC/parent at close time.
328	    pub f: Box<LuaProto>,
329	    /// C: prev — enclosing FuncState (raw pointer in C; owned Box here).
330	    pub prev: Option<Box<FuncState>>,
331	    /// C: bl — innermost active block
332	    pub bl: Option<Box<BlockCnt>>,
333	    /// C: pc — next bytecode position to emit
334	    pub pc: i32,
335	    /// C: lasttarget — pc of last 'jump label'
336	    pub lasttarget: i32,
337	    /// C: previousline — last line saved in lineinfo
338	    pub previousline: i32,
339	    /// C: nk — number of constants emitted
340	    pub nk: i32,
341	    /// C: np — number of nested prototypes emitted
342	    pub np: i32,
343	    /// C: nabslineinfo — number of absolute line-info records
344	    pub nabslineinfo: i32,
345	    /// C: firstlocal — index of first local var in dyd.actvar
346	    pub firstlocal: i32,
347	    /// C: firstlabel — index of first label in dyd.label
348	    pub firstlabel: i32,
349	    /// C: ndebugvars — entries in f.locvars
350	    pub ndebugvars: i16,
351	    /// C: nactvar — number of active locals
352	    pub nactvar: u8,
353	    /// C: nups — number of upvalues
354	    pub nups: u8,
355	    /// C: freereg — next free register
356	    pub freereg: u8,
357	    /// C: iwthabs — instructions since last absolute line info
358	    pub iwthabs: u8,
359	    /// C: needclose — function must close upvalues on return
360	    pub needclose: bool,
361	}
362	
363	// ── ConsControl ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
364	
365	/// C: ConsControl — state for parsing a table constructor.
366	/// PORT NOTE: C stores `expdesc *t` as a pointer to the caller's expdesc.
367	///   Rust stores a copy of the table descriptor; callers must sync back
368	///   if they mutate it. Phase B may restructure.
369	#[derive(Debug)]
370	pub struct ConsControl {
371	    /// C: v — last list item read
372	    pub v: ExprDesc,
373	    /// C: *t — table descriptor (copied; see PORT NOTE above)
374	    pub t: ExprDesc,
375	    /// C: nh — total number of record elements
376	    pub nh: i32,
377	    /// C: na — number of array elements already stored
378	    pub na: i32,
379	    /// C: tostore — array elements pending store
380	    pub tostore: i32,
381	}
382	
383	// ── LhsAssign ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
384	
385	/// C: LHS_assign — chain of assignment left-hand-side variables.
386	/// In C: stack-allocated, chained via raw `*prev`. In Rust: `Option<Box<...>>`.
387	#[derive(Debug)]
388	pub struct LhsAssign {
389	    /// C: *prev — previous (outer) assignment target; None at head.
390	    pub prev: Option<Box<LhsAssign>>,
391	    /// C: v — the variable being assigned
392	    pub v: ExprDesc,
393	}
394	
395	// ── Unary / binary operator enums ───────────────────────────────────────────
396	// C: UnOpr and BinOpr from lcode.h; defined locally here for Phase A.
397	// TODO(port): unify with lua_code::UnOpr / BinOpr when lua-code lands.
398	
399	/// C: UnOpr
400	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
401	pub enum UnOpr {
402	    Minus,    // OPR_MINUS
403	    BNot,     // OPR_BNOT
404	    Not,      // OPR_NOT
405	    Len,      // OPR_LEN
406	    NoUnOpr,  // OPR_NOUNOPR
407	}
408	
409	/// C: BinOpr
410	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
411	pub enum BinOpr {
412	    Add,     // OPR_ADD
413	    Sub,     // OPR_SUB
414	    Mul,     // OPR_MUL

126	// ── ExprKind ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	
128	/// C: expkind — the kind of a deferred expression or variable.
129	/// Variants correspond exactly to the C enum in lparser.h.
130	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
131	pub enum ExprKind {
132	    Void,       // VVOID: empty expression list
133	    Nil,        // VNIL: constant nil
134	    True,       // VTRUE: constant true
135	    False,      // VFALSE: constant false
136	    K,          // VK: constant in k[]; info = index
137	    KFlt,       // VKFLT: float constant; u.nval
138	    KInt,       // VKINT: integer constant; u.ival
139	    KStr,       // VKSTR: string constant; u.strval
140	    NonReloc,   // VNONRELOC: value in fixed register; info = reg
141	    Local,      // VLOCAL: local variable; u.var.ridx, u.var.vidx
142	    UpVal,      // VUPVAL: upvalue; info = upvalue index
143	    Const,      // VCONST: compile-time const; info = absolute actvar index
144	    Indexed,    // VINDEXED: indexed by reg key; u.ind.t, u.ind.idx
145	    IndexUp,    // VINDEXUP: indexed upvalue; u.ind.t, u.ind.idx
146	    IndexI,     // VINDEXI: indexed by int; u.ind.t, u.ind.idx
147	    IndexStr,   // VINDEXSTR: indexed by string; u.ind.t, u.ind.idx
148	    Jmp,        // VJMP: test/comparison; info = jump instruction pc
149	    Reloc,      // VRELOC: result in any register; info = instruction pc
150	    Call,       // VCALL: function call; info = instruction pc
151	    VarArg,     // VVARARG: vararg; info = instruction pc
152	}
153	
154	impl ExprKind {
155	    /// C: hasmultret(k) — ((k) == VCALL || (k) == VVARARG)
156	    #[inline]
157	    pub fn has_mult_ret(self) -> bool {
158	        matches!(self, ExprKind::Call | ExprKind::VarArg)
159	    }
160	
161	    /// C: vkisvar(k) — VLOCAL <= k <= VINDEXSTR
162	    #[inline]
163	    pub fn is_var(self) -> bool {
164	        matches!(
165	            self,
166	            ExprKind::Local
167	                | ExprKind::UpVal
168	                | ExprKind::Const
169	                | ExprKind::Indexed
170	                | ExprKind::IndexUp
171	                | ExprKind::IndexI
172	                | ExprKind::IndexStr
173	        )
174	    }
175	
176	    /// C: vkisindexed(k) — VINDEXED <= k <= VINDEXSTR
177	    #[inline]
178	    pub fn is_indexed(self) -> bool {
179	        matches!(
180	            self,
181	            ExprKind::Indexed | ExprKind::IndexUp | ExprKind::IndexI | ExprKind::IndexStr
182	        )
183	    }
184	}

186	// ── ExprPayload ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
187	
188	/// C: the `u` union inside `expdesc`.
189	/// PORT NOTE: C uses a union; all arms share memory. Rust keeps all fields in
190	///   one struct for Phase A simplicity. Phase B may refactor to a proper enum.
191	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
192	pub struct ExprPayload {
193	    /// C: u.ival — for VKINT
194	    pub ival: i64,
195	    /// C: u.nval — for VKFLT
196	    pub nval: f64,
197	    /// C: u.strval — for VKSTR
198	    pub strval: Option<GcRef<LuaString>>,
199	    /// C: u.info — for VK/VNONRELOC/VUPVAL/VCONST/VJMP/VRELOC/VCALL/VVARARG
200	    pub info: i32,
201	    /// C: u.ind.idx — register or K index for VINDEXED/VINDEXUP/VINDEXI/VINDEXSTR
202	    pub ind_idx: i16,
203	    /// C: u.ind.t — table register or upvalue index
204	    pub ind_t: u8,
205	    /// C: u.var.ridx — register holding the local variable (VLOCAL)
206	    pub var_ridx: u8,
207	    /// C: u.var.vidx — compiler index in actvar.arr (VLOCAL)
208	    pub var_vidx: u16,
209	}

215	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
216	pub struct ExprDesc {
217	    pub k: ExprKind,
218	    pub u: ExprPayload,
219	    /// C: e.t — patch list for 'exit when true'; NO_JUMP if none.

delta.text: Both branches work. Ver

ifying no temporary instrumentation was added — I added none.

**
Root cause**: The lua-parse codegen for `if`/`elseif`/`else` (`test
_then_block` at lib.rs:2895 and `
ifstat` at lib.rs:2946) had every jump-patch call stubbed out as `TODO(port)
`. `cg_emit_order` correctly emitted `OP_LTI`/`OP_LT` followed by `OP
_JMP` with offset `NO_JUMP` (-1), but `goi
ftrue`/`patchtohere`/`concat` were never called. At runtime the VM took
the conditional jump on the comparison and computed `pc = pc + (-1) + 1 = pc` —

infinite loop.

**Fix**: Added Phase-A local implementations in crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs of `cg_jump`,
`cg_get_jump`, `cg_fix_jump`, `cg_get_label`, `cg_concat`, `cg_patch_list`, `cg_patch_to_here`, `cg
_negate_condition`, `cg_go_if_true` (mirroring `lcode.c`'s `luaK_*` family
), and wired them into `test_then_block` (negate the comparison's k b
it, emit jump-over-then on else/elseif, patch the jf

to the else label) and `ifstat` (patch escapelist to end of if).

**New behavior**: `if 1 < 2 then print("yes") else print("
no") end` prints `yes`; `if 2 < 1 then ... end` prints `no`. Original infinite-loop blocker is gone.

content.text:
Both branches work. Verifying no temporary instrumentation was added — I added none.

**Root cause**: The lua-parse codegen for `if`/`elseif`/`else` (`test_then_block` at lib.rs:2895 and `ifstat` at lib.rs:2946) had every jump-patch call stubbed out as `TODO(port)`. `cg_emit_order` correctly emitted `OP_LTI`/`OP_LT` followed by `OP_JMP` with offset `NO_JUMP` (-1), but `goiftrue`/`patchtohere`/`concat` were never called. At runtime the VM took the conditional jump on the comparison and computed `pc = pc + (-1) + 1 = pc` — infinite loop.

**Fix**: Added Phase-A local implementations in crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs of `cg_jump`, `cg_get_jump`, `cg_fix_jump`, `cg_get_label`, `cg_concat`, `cg_patch_list`, `cg_patch_to_here`, `cg_negate_condition`, `cg_go_if_true` (mirroring `lcode.c`'s `luaK_*` family), and wired them into `test_then_block` (negate the comparison's k bit, emit jump-over-then on else/elseif, patch the jf to the else label) and `ifstat` (patch escapelist to end of if).

**New behavior**: `if 1 < 2 then print("yes") else print("no") end` prints `yes`; `if 2 < 1 then ... end` prints `no`. Original infinite-loop blocker is gone.
